fields.summary: Node label manager API and storage implementations
(Clone of YARN-796) Allow for (admin) labels on nodes and resource-requests

status.description: The issue is open and ready for the assignee to start work on it.
status.name: Open

fields.description:
This JIRA includes APIs and storage implementations of node label manager,
NodeLabelManager is an abstract class used to manage labels of nodes in the cluster, it has APIs to query/modify
- Nodes according to given label
- Labels according to given hostname
- Add/remove labels
- Set labels of nodes in the cluster
- Persist/recover changes of labels/labels-on-nodes to/from storage

And it has two implementations to store modifications
- Memory based storage: It will not persist changes, so all labels will be lost when RM restart
- FileSystem based storage: It will persist/recover to/from FileSystem (like HDFS), and all labels and labels-on-nodes will be recovered upon RM restart

[~cwelch],
I replied your comment in YARN-796 here, I think it's an implementation detail of FileSystemNodeLabelManager:
bq. It looks like the FileSystemNodeLabelManager will just append changes to the edit log forever, until it is restarted, is that correct? If so, a long-running cluster with lots of changes could result in a rather large edit log. I think every so many writes (N writes) a recovery should be "forced" to clean up the edit log and consolidate state (do a recover...)

It a good suggestion, but I think it's more like an enhancement to me. I roughly estimate, if we have 10,000 node label changes in one hour, average size of the label is 16 (8 for label and 8 for node), if we have the cluster running for one year, size of the editlog will be: {{10000 * 16 * 24 * 365 / 1024 / 1024}} MB = 1336 MB, according to existing HDFS read throughput (at least we can get 50MB / sec), it should be acceptable to me if restart a RM ran for a whole year and cost about 30s extra time.

I agree that periodically create a new mirror and cleanup editlog is better than this, we can do it if we have other high priority problems addressed.

Thanks,
Wangda

Started reviewing. I wish I saw the patch-size before I did :)

Comments on the patch:

h3. Overarching comments

Why is all of this code in yarn-common and not in server?

I think what you refer as Labels in many places are really just "Known labels" : labels that the system knows as valid. We should rename it so.

h3. Source code comments

Unless we really want this to pluggable, which I doubt, YarnConfiguration.NODE_LABEL_MANAGER_CLS and NodeLabelManagerFactory should just dropped

AddLabelsEvent
 - partitions -> labels

If we change labels to be knownLabels, the following needs to change
 - {Add|Remove}Labels is really {Add|Remove}KnownLabels.
 - NodeLabelConfiguration.LABELS_KEY
 - NodeLabelsManager.existingLabels -> existingKnownLabels

NodeLabelConfiguration
 - Configuring the nodes to labels is really cumbersome to drive using so many config properties. We need a better solution.

YarnConfiguration
 - LABEL_PREFIX/labels should NODE_LABELS_PREFIX/node-labels.
 - FS_NODE_LABEL_STORE_URI -> FS_NODE_LABELS_STORE_URI.
 - fs.store.uri -> fs-store.uri
 - fs.retry-policy-spec -> fs-store.retry-policy-spec

Package name org.apache.hadoop.yarn.label
 - Rename to org.apache.hadoop.yarn.node-labels

NodeLabelManager
 - Rename to NodeLabelsManager. Similarly the sub-classes.
 - We can decouple the sub-classes from NodeLabelsManager by making them stores - the inheritance hierarchy is going to be really hard to maintain going forward. We can have MemoryStore, FileSystemStore etc. I've tried this and is easily possible. That way we can clearly demarcate what the store owns and what the label-manager owns. NodeLabelManager is already clear in this regard, but the stores aren't - we can make NodeLabelManager keep a handle to the store and vice-versa if needed.
 - Many methods don't really need to throw IOException like in getActiveNodesByLabel(String label).
 - queueToLabels and queueToResource shouldn't really stay here. Queue -> node-labels is just one way of configuring and using node-labels. This should ideally move to a queue representation - some sort of a QueueToNodeLabelsManager or to Queue itself.
 - Once we do the above, testGetQueueResource() should also be decoupled from regular node-label tests.
 - Removing a known-label is supported for end-users (not clear as I haven't seen all patches yet)? Is it okay to just go and drop the label from all queues and all nodes? Not sure if that is acceptable for all sites.
 - Instead of multiple multi-level maps, you can create an inner "NodeLabel" class which has a name, the list of nodes and active-nodes that correspond to it.
 - What is the difference between a node and an active node in the above context?
 - Similarly a nested "Node" can encapsulate the corresponding list of labels and the total resource. Also the NO_LABEL can be completely hidden in this class.
 - We should use NodeIds everywhere instead of string hosts? Logically, labels can be set for two different NMs running on a single physical host.
 - Reorder all the methods so that (1) all additions are together, all removals together (2) all getters() are together, all 'writable' methods are together.
 - The state-machine in NodeLabelsManager isn't really a state-machine and is not adding anything. What you just need is a dispatcher to asynchronously store/modify node-labels. It made sense for RMStateStore as that store could become unavailable etc. Do we have similar requirements here?

FileSystemStore
 - You are deleting old-mirror file twice. 
 - Overall, we can invent a file-based scheme or just bite the bullet and use some sort of leveldb-store. Thoughts for future.

h3. Tests

Is SyncDispatcher same as InlineDispatcher?

TestNodeLabelManager
 - DumbNodeLabelManager -> DummyNodeLabelManager :)

Hi [~vinodkv],
bq. Started reviewing. I wish I saw the patch-size before I did 
Thanks for reviwing this giant patch :)

--- Overarching part ---

bq. Why is all of this code in yarn-common and not in server?
Because we need yarn-client access this too (edit labels in storage directly when RM is offline), so I put these code to common dependency between yarn-client and yarn-server-resourcemanager.

bq. I think what you refer as Labels in many places are really just "Known labels" : labels that the system knows as valid. We should rename it so.
I'm not quite agree about this, we can say "remove a known label", but we cannoy say "add a known label" -- a label being add is unknown actually. And IMO, "existing" is very similar to known, if a label is existing in NodeLabelManager, that is a "known" label.
and for
bq. If we change labels to be knownLabels, the following needs to change ... {Add|Remove}Labels is really {Add|Remove}
I suggest to change {{addLabels}} to {{addNodeLabels}} because we may support more different kind of labels in the future, change {{removeLabels}} to {{removeExistingLabels}}, and leave {{NodeLabelsManager.existingLabels}} unchanged.
Does this sound good to you?

--- NodeLabelsManager ---

bq. Unless we really want this to pluggable, which I doubt, YarnConfiguration.NODE_LABEL_MANAGER_CLS and NodeLabelManagerFactory should just dropped
Addressed

bq. NodeLabelConfiguration
Sorry to confuse you, originally as you said, this is to configure labels by configuration file.
Now this is used to configure by using "yarn rmadmin -loadLabelsConfigFile node-labels.xml". I thought it might be useful for user want to configure a lot of labels but don't want enter long- command.

bq. YarnConfiguration ... LABEL_PREFIX/labels should NODE_LABELS_PREFIX/node-labels.
Addressed

bq. Package name org.apache.hadoop.yarn.label
Addressed

bq. Package name org.apache.hadoop.yarn.label, Rename to org.apache.hadoop.yarn.node-labels
Since "-" isn't a valid package name, I renamed it to {{org.apache.hadoop.yarn.nodelabels}}

bq. Rename to NodeLabelsManager. Similarly the sub-classes.
Addressed

bq. We can decouple the sub-classes from NodeLabelsManager by making them stores
Good suggestion, done.

bq. Many methods don't really need to throw IOException like in getActiveNodesByLabel(String label).
Done

bq. Removing a known-label is supported for end-users (not clear as I haven't seen all patches yet)? Is it okay to just go and drop the label from all queues and all nodes?
Yes, end-user can remove a known-label.
I think it should be fine to remove from all nodes, it's very similar to removing a key from a map, its value will also be removed.
But I think we shouldn't remove labels from queues, because labels of queue is defined in capacity-scheduler.xml, if we just remove them in memory. RM will fail to restart (doesn't have the known label, but queue still use it).
I added checking in removeNodeLabels, when a queue is using a label to be removed, the removeNodeLabels will fail.

bq. queueToLabels and queueToResource shouldn't really stay here. Queue -> node-labels is just one way of configuring and using node-labels. This should ideally move to a queue representation - some sort of a QueueToNodeLabelsManager or to Queue itself.
bq. Once we do the above, testGetQueueResource() should also be decoupled from regular node-label tests.
queueToResources depends on some internal data structure in NodeLabelManager, it will be updated when (de)activateNode/change-labels-on-node. I think it will be hard to become a separated class.

bq. What is the difference between a node and an active node in the above context?
Active node is a NM already registered to RM. It is possible that a user specify labels on node, but the node hasn't NMs on it.
Active nodes is a subset of nodes in NodeLabelsManager, it is used to calculate realtime resource, like resource on queues.

bq. Similarly a nested "Node" can encapsulate the corresponding list of labels and the total resource. Also the NO_LABEL can be completely hidden in this class.
Done, also for labels/resource on queue.
Removed public from NO_LABEL.

bq. We should use NodeIds everywhere instead of string hosts? Logically, labels can be set for two different NMs running on a single physical host.
Set labels on NodeId instead of node will cause a problem: this assumes NM is running, so we need start RM before setting labels on NodeIds. We've discussed a scenario admin should be able to set labels when RM is offline.

bq. The state-machine in NodeLabelsManager isn't really a state-machine and is not adding anything.
Removed state-machine

--- FileSystemNodeLabelManager ---

bq. You are deleting old-mirror file twice.
I think you mean following code,
{code}
    // Move mirror to mirror.old
    if (fs.exists(mirrorPath)) {
      fs.delete(oldMirrorPath, false);
      fs.rename(mirrorPath, oldMirrorPath);
    }
    
    // move mirror.writing to mirror
    fs.rename(writingMirrorPath, mirrorPath);
    fs.delete(writingMirrorPath, false);
    
    // remove mirror.old
    fs.delete(oldMirrorPath, false);
{code}
The two delete is not on the same file. This piece of code is used to avoid data lose when RM process crash.
As I commented in the code:
{code}
    /*
     * Steps of recover
     * 1) Read from last mirror (from mirror or mirror.old)
     * 2) Read from last edit log, and apply such edit log
     * 3) Write new mirror to mirror.writing
     * 4) Rename mirror to mirror.old
     * 5) Move mirror.writing to mirror
     * 6) Remove mirror.old
     * 7) Remove edit log and create a new empty edit log 
     */
{code}
It is possible that mirror and mirror.old file existing at the same time when RM crashes between step 5)/6). The previous delete is to handle this case, when trying to do step 4), but the mirror.old existed, we should remove it before rename.

bq. Overall, we can invent a file-based scheme or just bite the bullet and use some sort of leveldb-store. Thoughts for future
+1, a structured storage should be better than today.

--- Tests ---
bq. Is SyncDispatcher same as InlineDispatcher?
Yes, it is. Replaced it with InlineDispatcher and removed the SyncDispatcher

bq. DumbNodeLabelManager -> DummyNodeLabelManager
Renamed :) Thanks for reminding,

Re-generating patch, will upload soon

Thanks again,
Wangda
Attached new patch

-re I suggest to change addLabels to addNodeLabels because we may support more different kind of labels in the future, change removeLabels to removeExistingLabels, and leave NodeLabelsManager.existingLabels unchanged.

I thought we'd setteled on just adding "Node" to the names which did not have it, so addNodeLables, removeNodeLabels, etc.  I don't think "Existing" and "Known" are particularly helpful, the concern was to distinguish these as "NodeLabel" operations, to leave room in the future for other kinds of nodes.

Also, with the refactor to a "store" type and dropping the configuration option, do we still have a way to specify something other than the hdfs store?

wrt leveldb - we ended up with hdfs for the ha case, I think anything we do should be distributed, not local - so zookeeper, hbase, etc.
...other kinds of labels..., rather

Still reading up the comments and the latest patch. But some quick replies

bq. Because we need yarn-client access this too (edit labels in storage directly when RM is offline), so I put these code to common dependency between yarn-client and yarn-server-resourcemanager.
Once you have the store abstraction, this will be less of a problem? Clearly NodeLabelsManager is not something that the client needs access to?

bq. I'm not quite agree about this, we can say "remove a known label", but we cannoy say "add a known label" – a label being add is unknown actually. And IMO, "existing" is very similar to known, if a label is existing in NodeLabelManager, that is a "known" label.
What I really want is to convey is that these are just system recognized nodelabels as opposed to node-lables that are actually mapped against a node. How about {{addToNodeLabelsCollection()}}, {{removeFromNodeLabelsCollection()}}, {{addLabelsToNode()}} and {{removeLabelsFromNode()}}. The point about {{addToNodeLabelsCollection()}} is that it clearly conveys that there is a NodeLabelsCollection - a set of node-labels known by the system.

Hi [~vinodkv] and [~cwelch],
Thanks for reply! Still working on handling your last comments, will upload patch soon.

Regarding method name of NodeLabelManager, I think following suggestion make sense to me:
bq. What I really want is to convey is that these are just system recognized nodelabels as opposed to node-lables that are actually mapped against a node. How about addToNodeLabelsCollection(), removeFromNodeLabelsCollection(), addLabelsToNode() and removeLabelsFromNode(). The point about addToNodeLabelsCollection() is that it clearly conveys that there is a NodeLabelsCollection - a set of node-labels known by the system.

And regarding
bq. Once you have the store abstraction, this will be less of a problem? Clearly NodeLabelsManager is not something that the client needs access to?
I think it still has problem: Even if we have store abstraction, we still need some logic to guarantee labels being added are valid (e.g. we need check if a label existed in collection, and label existed in node when we trying to remove some labels from a node). That makes we need put a greater chunk of logic to the store abstraction -- it isn't a simple store abstraction if we do this.
I suggest to keep it in common to make node label major logic are live together.

Thanks,
Wangda

Not to dither about names - but "Collection" is still not terribly clear to me (overly generic), I was thinking previously about "Cluster" as the differentiator, so:

addToClusterNodeLabels(), removeFromClusterNodeLabels(), addLabelsToNode() and removeLabelsFromNode(). 

I think this conveys the different notions of what the operations are applying to in a pretty clear way.  Thoughts?

bq. I think it still has problem: Even if we have store abstraction, we still need some logic to guarantee labels being added are valid (e.g. we need check if a label existed in collection, and label existed in node when we trying to remove some labels from a node).
Then that validation code needs to get pulled out in a common layer. My goal it not put the entire NodelabelsManager in yarn-common - it just doesn't belong there.

bq. How about addToNodeLabelsCollection(), removeFromNodeLabelsCollection(), addLabelsToNode() and removeLabelsFromNode()
bq. addToClusterNodeLabels(), removeFromClusterNodeLabels(), addLabelsToNode() and removeLabelsFromNode(). 
[~leftnoteasy], [~cwelch], I'm okay with either of the above. Or should we call it {{ClusterNodeLabelsCollection}}? :)

I feel the "Cluster" is almost a super set of "Collection". I prefer what [~cwelch] suggested set of method name.
Maybe {{ClusterNodeLabelsCollection}} is slightly clear than {{ClusterNodeLabels}}, but its name is too long I think :)
Addressed comments from [~vinodkv], and [~cwelch]. Attached new patch against trunk

[~vinodkv], thanks for your comments! All comments make sense to me.
Attached a new patch.

Wangda

[~cwelch],
I replied your comment in YARN-796 here, I think it's an implementation detail of FileSystemNodeLabelManager:
bq. It looks like the FileSystemNodeLabelManager will just append changes to the edit log forever, until it is restarted, is that correct? If so, a long-running cluster with lots of changes could result in a rather large edit log. I think every so many writes (N writes) a recovery should be "forced" to clean up the edit log and consolidate state (do a recover...)

It a good suggestion, but I think it's more like an enhancement to me. I roughly estimate, if we have 10,000 node label changes in one hour, average size of the label is 16 (8 for label and 8 for node), if we have the cluster running for one year, size of the editlog will be: {{10000 * 16 * 24 * 365 / 1024 / 1024}} MB = 1336 MB, according to existing HDFS read throughput (at least we can get 50MB / sec), it should be acceptable to me if restart a RM ran for a whole year and cost about 30s extra time.

I agree that periodically create a new mirror and cleanup editlog is better than this, we can do it if we have other high priority problems addressed.

Thanks,
Wangda

Started reviewing. I wish I saw the patch-size before I did :)

Comments on the patch:

h3. Overarching comments

Why is all of this code in yarn-common and not in server?

I think what you refer as Labels in many places are really just "Known labels" : labels that the system knows as valid. We should rename it so.

h3. Source code comments

Unless we really want this to pluggable, which I doubt, YarnConfiguration.NODE_LABEL_MANAGER_CLS and NodeLabelManagerFactory should just dropped

AddLabelsEvent
 - partitions -> labels

If we change labels to be knownLabels, the following needs to change
 - {Add|Remove}Labels is really {Add|Remove}KnownLabels.
 - NodeLabelConfiguration.LABELS_KEY
 - NodeLabelsManager.existingLabels -> existingKnownLabels

NodeLabelConfiguration
 - Configuring the nodes to labels is really cumbersome to drive using so many config properties. We need a better solution.

YarnConfiguration
 - LABEL_PREFIX/labels should NODE_LABELS_PREFIX/node-labels.
 - FS_NODE_LABEL_STORE_URI -> FS_NODE_LABELS_STORE_URI.
 - fs.store.uri -> fs-store.uri
 - fs.retry-policy-spec -> fs-store.retry-policy-spec

Package name org.apache.hadoop.yarn.label
 - Rename to org.apache.hadoop.yarn.node-labels

NodeLabelManager
 - Rename to NodeLabelsManager. Similarly the sub-classes.
 - We can decouple the sub-classes from NodeLabelsManager by making them stores - the inheritance hierarchy is going to be really hard to maintain going forward. We can have MemoryStore, FileSystemStore etc. I've tried this and is easily possible. That way we can clearly demarcate what the store owns and what the label-manager owns. NodeLabelManager is already clear in this regard, but the stores aren't - we can make NodeLabelManager keep a handle to the store and vice-versa if needed.
 - Many methods don't really need to throw IOException like in getActiveNodesByLabel(String label).
 - queueToLabels and queueToResource shouldn't really stay here. Queue -> node-labels is just one way of configuring and using node-labels. This should ideally move to a queue representation - some sort of a QueueToNodeLabelsManager or to Queue itself.
 - Once we do the above, testGetQueueResource() should also be decoupled from regular node-label tests.
 - Removing a known-label is supported for end-users (not clear as I haven't seen all patches yet)? Is it okay to just go and drop the label from all queues and all nodes? Not sure if that is acceptable for all sites.
 - Instead of multiple multi-level maps, you can create an inner "NodeLabel" class which has a name, the list of nodes and active-nodes that correspond to it.
 - What is the difference between a node and an active node in the above context?
 - Similarly a nested "Node" can encapsulate the corresponding list of labels and the total resource. Also the NO_LABEL can be completely hidden in this class.
 - We should use NodeIds everywhere instead of string hosts? Logically, labels can be set for two different NMs running on a single physical host.
 - Reorder all the methods so that (1) all additions are together, all removals together (2) all getters() are together, all 'writable' methods are together.
 - The state-machine in NodeLabelsManager isn't really a state-machine and is not adding anything. What you just need is a dispatcher to asynchronously store/modify node-labels. It made sense for RMStateStore as that store could become unavailable etc. Do we have similar requirements here?

FileSystemStore
 - You are deleting old-mirror file twice. 
 - Overall, we can invent a file-based scheme or just bite the bullet and use some sort of leveldb-store. Thoughts for future.

h3. Tests

Is SyncDispatcher same as InlineDispatcher?

TestNodeLabelManager
 - DumbNodeLabelManager -> DummyNodeLabelManager :)

Hi [~vinodkv],
bq. Started reviewing. I wish I saw the patch-size before I did 
Thanks for reviwing this giant patch :)

--- Overarching part ---

bq. Why is all of this code in yarn-common and not in server?
Because we need yarn-client access this too (edit labels in storage directly when RM is offline), so I put these code to common dependency between yarn-client and yarn-server-resourcemanager.

bq. I think what you refer as Labels in many places are really just "Known labels" : labels that the system knows as valid. We should rename it so.
I'm not quite agree about this, we can say "remove a known label", but we cannoy say "add a known label" -- a label being add is unknown actually. And IMO, "existing" is very similar to known, if a label is existing in NodeLabelManager, that is a "known" label.
and for
bq. If we change labels to be knownLabels, the following needs to change ... {Add|Remove}Labels is really {Add|Remove}
I suggest to change {{addLabels}} to {{addNodeLabels}} because we may support more different kind of labels in the future, change {{removeLabels}} to {{removeExistingLabels}}, and leave {{NodeLabelsManager.existingLabels}} unchanged.
Does this sound good to you?

--- NodeLabelsManager ---

bq. Unless we really want this to pluggable, which I doubt, YarnConfiguration.NODE_LABEL_MANAGER_CLS and NodeLabelManagerFactory should just dropped
Addressed

bq. NodeLabelConfiguration
Sorry to confuse you, originally as you said, this is to configure labels by configuration file.
Now this is used to configure by using "yarn rmadmin -loadLabelsConfigFile node-labels.xml". I thought it might be useful for user want to configure a lot of labels but don't want enter long- command.

bq. YarnConfiguration ... LABEL_PREFIX/labels should NODE_LABELS_PREFIX/node-labels.
Addressed

bq. Package name org.apache.hadoop.yarn.label
Addressed

bq. Package name org.apache.hadoop.yarn.label, Rename to org.apache.hadoop.yarn.node-labels
Since "-" isn't a valid package name, I renamed it to {{org.apache.hadoop.yarn.nodelabels}}

bq. Rename to NodeLabelsManager. Similarly the sub-classes.
Addressed

bq. We can decouple the sub-classes from NodeLabelsManager by making them stores
Good suggestion, done.

bq. Many methods don't really need to throw IOException like in getActiveNodesByLabel(String label).
Done

bq. Removing a known-label is supported for end-users (not clear as I haven't seen all patches yet)? Is it okay to just go and drop the label from all queues and all nodes?
Yes, end-user can remove a known-label.
I think it should be fine to remove from all nodes, it's very similar to removing a key from a map, its value will also be removed.
But I think we shouldn't remove labels from queues, because labels of queue is defined in capacity-scheduler.xml, if we just remove them in memory. RM will fail to restart (doesn't have the known label, but queue still use it).
I added checking in removeNodeLabels, when a queue is using a label to be removed, the removeNodeLabels will fail.

bq. queueToLabels and queueToResource shouldn't really stay here. Queue -> node-labels is just one way of configuring and using node-labels. This should ideally move to a queue representation - some sort of a QueueToNodeLabelsManager or to Queue itself.
bq. Once we do the above, testGetQueueResource() should also be decoupled from regular node-label tests.
queueToResources depends on some internal data structure in NodeLabelManager, it will be updated when (de)activateNode/change-labels-on-node. I think it will be hard to become a separated class.

bq. What is the difference between a node and an active node in the above context?
Active node is a NM already registered to RM. It is possible that a user specify labels on node, but the node hasn't NMs on it.
Active nodes is a subset of nodes in NodeLabelsManager, it is used to calculate realtime resource, like resource on queues.

bq. Similarly a nested "Node" can encapsulate the corresponding list of labels and the total resource. Also the NO_LABEL can be completely hidden in this class.
Done, also for labels/resource on queue.
Removed public from NO_LABEL.

bq. We should use NodeIds everywhere instead of string hosts? Logically, labels can be set for two different NMs running on a single physical host.
Set labels on NodeId instead of node will cause a problem: this assumes NM is running, so we need start RM before setting labels on NodeIds. We've discussed a scenario admin should be able to set labels when RM is offline.

bq. The state-machine in NodeLabelsManager isn't really a state-machine and is not adding anything.
Removed state-machine

--- FileSystemNodeLabelManager ---

bq. You are deleting old-mirror file twice.
I think you mean following code,
{code}
    // Move mirror to mirror.old
    if (fs.exists(mirrorPath)) {
      fs.delete(oldMirrorPath, false);
      fs.rename(mirrorPath, oldMirrorPath);
    }
    
    // move mirror.writing to mirror
    fs.rename(writingMirrorPath, mirrorPath);
    fs.delete(writingMirrorPath, false);
    
    // remove mirror.old
    fs.delete(oldMirrorPath, false);
{code}
The two delete is not on the same file. This piece of code is used to avoid data lose when RM process crash.
As I commented in the code:
{code}
    /*
     * Steps of recover
     * 1) Read from last mirror (from mirror or mirror.old)
     * 2) Read from last edit log, and apply such edit log
     * 3) Write new mirror to mirror.writing
     * 4) Rename mirror to mirror.old
     * 5) Move mirror.writing to mirror
     * 6) Remove mirror.old
     * 7) Remove edit log and create a new empty edit log 
     */
{code}
It is possible that mirror and mirror.old file existing at the same time when RM crashes between step 5)/6). The previous delete is to handle this case, when trying to do step 4), but the mirror.old existed, we should remove it before rename.

bq. Overall, we can invent a file-based scheme or just bite the bullet and use some sort of leveldb-store. Thoughts for future
+1, a structured storage should be better than today.
